name: CLIP (OpenAI Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); original OpenAI CLIP; GPU recommended

performance.notes: ~20ms per image encoding on GPU; very fast for embedding generation

agent_readiness.notes: OpenAI's foundational multimodal embedding model. Encodes images and text into shared vector space — images and text with matching semantics are nearby. Zero-shot image classification, image search, cross-modal retrieval. SigLIP (Google's improved variant) now preferred for quality. HuggingFace open-clip offers many CLIP variants. Foundation for many later models (DALL-E, Stable Diffusion guidance). Used in multimodal search and content moderation.

gotchas: Original CLIP superseded by SigLIP and larger open-clip variants for quality
Not a generative model — embeddings only (no image captioning)
Zero-shot classification degrades with unusual/domain-specific classes
GPU significantly faster than CPU for batch processing
best_when: Building image embedding indexes for vector search
avoid_when: When SigLIP or OpenCLIP provide better quality for the use case
summary: CLIP is OpenAI's foundational model that aligns image and text in a shared embedding space. Enables zero-shot image classification, image search, and cross-modal retrieval. Used in multimodal vector search, content moderation, and as guidance for generative models. Now superseded by SigLIP and OpenCLIP variants for quality. Essential building block for multimodal AI applications.
